description: Ralph Loop — an autonomous, bench-gated coding agent tooling submodule. Reads a label-driven GitHub Issues backlog, runs on a local 3-hour scheduler, builds each story TDD off-target against a fakeable HAL, auto-merges AFK stories to develop and parks HIL stories at awaiting-bench, checkpoints via Handoff instead of compacting, and never touches main. Converted from GitHub issues #2–#12 (parent PRD #1).

userStories.title: Config: .ralph.yml schema + validation (ralph --check-config)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want .ralph.yml validated against a shipped JSON-schema before Ralph does anything, so that a malformed or missing config fails loud instead of Ralph guessing defaults.
acceptanceCriteria: Ship a JSON-schema for .ralph.yml in the repo covering: version, ordered named gating steps (name + run), branching (base default develop, branch_pattern, afk_merge), limits (max_attempts default 3, circuit_breaker default 2), notify (GitHub handle)
A documented sample .ralph.yml validates successfully
ralph --check-config exits 0 and prints a summary for a valid config
ralph --check-config exits non-zero and reports the offending field(s) for malformed configs (missing gating, bad afk_merge, wrong types)
A missing .ralph.yml fails loud rather than assuming defaults
The canonical state:/type:/prio: label scheme is mandated and not overridable in config
Tests cover good and malformed config fixtures
Tests pass
userStories.notes: GitHub issue #2. No dependencies. See ADR-0001.

userStories.title: Skill: story-formatting skill (specialize to-issues) for canonical Ralph backlog
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a formatting skill (specializing to-issues) that emits stories in the canonical label + body shape, so that authoring a well-formed backlog Ralph can read is easy.
acceptanceCriteria: The skill emits issues carrying the canonical state:/type:/prio: labels and a Depends on: body line
Every story gets a ## Acceptance Criteria checklist; HIL stories additionally get a ## Bench Test Procedure section
Each story is classified AFK or HIL; design-decision-first work is emitted as a Blocker (kept out of state:ready), not a third type
An advisory sizing checklist is applied and proposes splits for human approval (no hard gate, no token estimator)
Terminology is standardized on HIL, not HITL
Example runs produce well-formed issues the selection engine can consume
userStories.notes: GitHub issue #3. ready-for-human: judgment-heavy LLM behavior, verified by example rather than CI. No dependencies.

userStories.title: Selection: backlog dry-run (scan, normalize, print next action)
userStories.description: As Ralph, I want to scan the backlog and print the next action (resume #N / start #N / no-work / halt) without doing any work, driven by a pure selection engine, so that the crown-jewel decision logic is deterministic and unit-testable.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph scans the live backlog via gh (REST/--json) and prints the next action, changing nothing
Selection is a pure function over a normalized story list (no network, no LLM), unit-tested with fixtures
Resume any state:in-progress story is chosen before any state:ready scan
Ordering is prio:N then lowest issue number (FIFO); state:blocked is skipped
An AFK dependency counts satisfied only when merged; a HIL dependency only when bench-verified (closed)
A story blocked by an unverified HIL story is never selected
Empty/all-blocked backlogs yield no-work
Tests pass
userStories.notes: GitHub issue #4. Depends on #2 (US-002). See ADR-0002.

userStories.title: Iteration: TDD off-target story implementation to green (agent prompt v1)
userStories.description: As Ralph, I want a single fresh-context iteration that takes a chosen story and implements it test-first up to a green local gate, committing WIP to the story branch but not merging or closing.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a chosen story, Ralph creates the correct branch off base per branch_pattern (e.g. ralph/<issue#>-slug)
Failing tests are written from the acceptance criteria before implementation (red → green)
Logic is tested off-target on the host against a fake/mock HAL
The configured gating steps run locally and must pass before the story counts
Gating output is kept low-verbosity
Work is committed to the story branch; base branch and ai-utils are untouched
The agent prompt (v1) is checked in and drives the iteration
Tests pass
userStories.notes: GitHub issue #5. Depends on #4 (US-004). See ADR-0003.

userStories.title: AFK completion: auto-merge to develop + close
userStories.description: As Ralph, for an AFK story I want to auto-merge to base once gating is green and then continue, so that fully-autonomous work keeps develop moving and unblocks dependents.
acceptanceCriteria: A green type:afk story is merged to base per the afk_merge policy
The issue is closed / marked Passing after merge
main is never touched
After completion Ralph proceeds to the next eligible story
A merged AFK story now satisfies dependents' Depends on: edges
Behavior is covered by tests with a mocked gh/git
Tests pass
userStories.notes: GitHub issue #6. Depends on #5 (US-005). See ADR-0001.

userStories.title: HIL completion: PR to develop + awaiting-bench
userStories.description: As Ralph, for a HIL story I want to open a PR to base, move it to state:awaiting-bench, and stop, so that the human bench-tests one clean diff in isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: A green type:hil story opens a PR to base and moves to state:awaiting-bench
Ralph never merges a HIL story
The PR is a clean diff off base so it can be bench-tested in isolation
A HIL story in state:awaiting-bench is not Passing and does not satisfy dependents' Depends on: edges
Whatever is host-testable is still covered by tests; only genuinely hardware-coupled behavior is deferred to the ## Bench Test Procedure
Behavior is covered by tests with a mocked gh/git
Tests pass
userStories.notes: GitHub issue #7. Depends on #5 (US-005). See ADR-0001, ADR-0003.

userStories.title: Handoff: no-compaction checkpoint + resume
userStories.description: As Ralph, I want to never compact context; when context fills I want to write a Handoff and terminate, so that the next iteration resumes the same story with clean context.
acceptanceCriteria: When context fills, the iteration writes a Handoff (issue comment + WIP branch commits) and terminates cleanly
The next iteration resumes the in-progress story from the Handoff and branch
Resume state is stored only in the superproject; base branch is untouched
A context-full checkpoint does not increment the failed-Attempt count
Context is never compacted
Resume/checkpoint behavior is covered by tests
Tests pass
userStories.notes: GitHub issue #8. Depends on #5 (US-005). See ADR-0004.

userStories.title: Failure handling + circuit breaker + needs-human
userStories.description: As Ralph, after a configurable number of failed attempts I want to block a story and move on, and when a second story blocks I want to halt and tag a human, so systemic failures get attention fast.
acceptanceCriteria: After limits.max_attempts (default 3) failed Attempts a story moves to state:blocked with one terse comment (a context-full checkpoint is not an Attempt)
Ralph then continues with other non-blocked eligible work
A second blocked story trips limits.circuit_breaker (default 2): loop halts, needs-human label applied, configured handle tagged
A story kicked back to state:ready (failed HIL bench test) is re-attempted with a new failing test on a fresh PR
Attempt/circuit-breaker counting is covered by tests
Tests pass
userStories.notes: GitHub issue #9. Depends on #5 (US-005). See ADR-0004.

userStories.title: Memory: nested AGENTS.md learnings (no progress.txt)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want two-tier memory: durable learnings in nested AGENTS.md and transient notes on the issue, so memory is cleanly tiered by durability with no progress.txt.
acceptanceCriteria: Ralph reads nearby nested AGENTS.md at the start of a story
Reusable learnings are promoted to the nearest AGENTS.md before completion
AGENTS.md files stay lean and module-local (no single growing global file)
Story-specific notes stay on the issue, not in AGENTS.md
No progress.txt is created or read
Tests pass
userStories.notes: GitHub issue #10. Depends on #5 (US-005). See ADR-0005.

userStories.title: Orchestration: ralph.sh tick loop (flock, resume-first, session-exit)
userStories.description: As Ralph, I want a robust unattended tick in ralph.sh that guards with flock, resumes first, works many eligible stories, and ends cleanly on no-work/halt or session-limit exhaustion.
acceptanceCriteria: Only one tick per superproject runs at a time (flock lockfile in .git/); an overlapping tick exits immediately
A tick resumes an in-progress story before scanning for new state:ready work
A tick works multiple eligible stories in sequence until no eligible work remains
Session-limit exhaustion is detected from the claude CLI exit; the current story is checkpointed via Handoff and the tick ends cleanly
Orchestration is covered by bats tests driving the script against mocked claude and gh on PATH
Tests pass
userStories.notes: GitHub issue #11. Depends on #6, #7, #8, #9 (US-006..US-009). See ADR-0002, ADR-0004.

userStories.title: Scheduler + install docs (sample timer, sample .ralph.yml, README)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ai-utils to ship a sample scheduler unit, a sample .ralph.yml, and an install README, so that installing the loop in a new superproject is quick.
acceptanceCriteria: A sample cron entry and/or systemd timer runs a tick every 3 hours
A documented sample .ralph.yml is shipped
An install README covers submodule setup, config placement, schedule install, and gh/Claude auth prerequisites
Following the README from a fresh superproject results in Ralph running on schedule
userStories.notes: GitHub issue #12. Depends on #11 (US-011). See ADR-0001.
